rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID FRANK PHILLIPS II
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/2/25 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 4/28/25 was of the notice of the amended petition given with the remote instructions to Mary Lindsay only. (A pleading form "proof of service" was also filed on 4/28/25, but it did not have a copy of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing with the remote appearance instructions attached to it.) (2) A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his own pleadings. [Prob. C. Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (3) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. Photocopies are not acceptable. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) Paragraph 5 of the presumptive "Declaration" filed by the attorney on 6/24/25 (but not e-filed until 6/25/25, past the deadline of 6/24/25 for providing a courtesy copy to the examiner) does not make sense. The first accounting, as amended on 8/11/22, showed the Chase account xx5177. The total assets were reported to be $2,812,331.85 at their carry value as of 12/31/21 and $3,091,587.12 at their (presumably) market value. (5) Now the version of the accounting attached as Exhibit C to the attorney's declaration filed on 6/24/25 appears to have an incorrect total of mutual funds/ETFs. (6) Receipts for prior distributions have not yet been filed. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.c.] (7) Reimbursements to any individual must indicate who the original payee(s) was/were. (8) Payments to a third party to prepare the accounting must be deducted from the executor's statutory compensation. (9) Far more than the FDIC insured amount is still being held at one bank. Deadline is 8/19/25 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CARL H ERNST JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Final Accounting As Prepared By Former Conservator And The Fiduciary Accounting Firm Including Response To Objections Of The Public Guardian
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been addressed: 1) No Notice of Hearing (Form GC-020) has been filed (Probate Code Section 1460). 2) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). 3) The petition is not verified by the former conservator (Probate Code Section 1021(b)(2)). 4) The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period. Photocopies are not acceptable. For computer-generated printouts, comply with Probate Code section 2620(c)(1) and CRC 7.575(b). (LRSF 14.34.D). 5) For the first account, all fiduciaries must also file original account statements showing the account balance on the date immediately preceding the date the fiduciary was appointed. Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2). (LRSF 14.34.D.1). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL JAMES O'GRADY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) Compensation for extraordinary services will be allowed before final distribution only when it appears likely that the estate will remain in probate for an unusually long time, whether due to litigation or other cause, or on a showing that present payment will benefit the beneficiaries or the estate (San Francisco Local Rule 14.55.A). 2) Only Olivia Lizotte verified the instant petition (CRC 7.103(b)). 3) The request for partial statutory fees to administrator is vague; there are two co-administrators. Are both receiving a partial payment (presumably half of the 50% requested)? Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEGACY ENHANCEMENT POOLED SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST ACCOUNT F/B/O MATTHEW ELLIOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/26/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The petition caption fails to identify all relief sought. [CRC 7.102.] (3) The first accounting period was to have been for the period 3/12/24 - 3/11/25, as ordered on 3/27/24. (4) Please explain the hotel stays reimbursed to Rhonda Elliott. (5) Should the prepaid funeral arrangement not be shown as an "asset" on hand at the end of the accounting? (6) The report does not include any information about the beneficiary, his health or general condition, special needs, ability to use weights or a gym, etc. (7) If time was billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. (8) Filing fees for this petition were $435.00, not $449.75. (9) Legal fees in an interim accounting are granted only when actually provided. Only a final accounting petition may include a request for anticipated additional fees to wrap up an estate. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/12/25. A revised proposed order should also be provided, which does not include a finding (assets on hand) in the ordering portion and has the correct footer for the order settling this first account, as well as the corrected legal fees and costs. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (An accounting for a Court-approved trust must conform with Probate Code Sect. 2620, which requires that an original statement verifying the beginning balance of assets be filed. [Probate Code Sect. 2620(c)(2).] It was only by looking through all the papers filed in the related Civil case that the examiner was able to determine how much the settlement amount was, which was the starting value of this PSNT.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL P. BARRETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/16/25 at Counsel's request due to recently filed objections. Other issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) There does not appear to be anything in the records clarifying who Molly Margaret Tucker is or confirming that she is the same person as Margaret Mary Tucker, if she is. (2) How old is the decedent's grand-nephew Charles Tucker? (3) Notice must be given to the minor and also to the person having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] (4) Detailed schedules of RECEIPTS and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] (5) The 50 shares of Wilscott Holdings Corp. and 160 shares of SSGA Active ETF TR SPDR were not on the I&A. Nor were any Fidelity Inherited Roth accounts, which are shown to be on hand. (6) If the statutory attorney fee is to be paid, should the remaining cash not be reduced? (7) How does the executor propose to pay any personal or fiduciary income tax? Please provide a (revised) proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/2/25. A copy of the objections has not yet been received by the examiner, so it, too, should be provided by the objector. (The time for filing creditor claims expired on 12/22/24, not 12/20/24: four months after Letters were issued.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SURVIVOR'S TRUST OF THE 2003 SCHOLTEN FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 4, 2003 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IMOGENE B. GIELING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/19/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Both mailed and published notice must clearly indicate that the will is lost. [LRSF 14.20.] (2) The heirs and beneficiaries listed in the petition and on Exhibit A to the third supplement filed on 6/18/25 need clarification. (It might help to show the heirs in a simple family tree.) Is Elizabeth Frey, who is identified as the daughter of Herbert Albert, different from Elizabeth Frey, who is identified as Decedent's daughter and the mother of great-nephews (?) Julian Redman Frey and Jeremy Chance Griffin? (3) The third supplement was not signed and verified by the proposed executor. [CRC 7.103(c).] Please provide a copy of the lost will to be attached to the revised proposed order [LRSF 14.20] and courtesy copies of the any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/5/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IHSAN BAKER DUJAILI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required. Status hearing on filing the final Inventory and Appraisal will be set for 11/18/25. No appearance will be necessary if the inventory and appraisal is filed at least two weeks prior. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 7/14/26. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTELITA B PASILLAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/29/25. Examiner's Notes sent to attorney with the following issues: 1) The Response to Probate Examiner Notes filed on 6/18/25 cannot be reviewed as it has not been verified by the petitioner. Please also note: a. A verified declaration of due must be filed regarding search for Evangeline Neroza. If she has been found, Petitioner must attempt to also obtain a declination to serve as administrator. b. Item 5a is incomplete and inaccurate. i. No allegation is made regarding whether Decedent was survived by a registered domestic partner. ii. No allegation is made regarding whether Decedent was survived by a child. iii. The declaration filed on 6/18/25 sates that Decedent was not survived [sic] the issue of a predeceased child. However, the same declaration shows that Decedent was survived by issue of a predeceased child (Kevin Neroza). c. Amendment filed on 4/25/25 requests bond in the amount of $275K, but no authority has been cited to allow bond less than the full value of the estate. Notice of Hearing has also not been filed on the filing of the amendment. i. Petitioner makes reference to a Zillow appraisal in the declaration filed on 6/18/25, however, what is relevant is the stated value of the estate in item 3d of the petition ($2M). ii. The notice must indicate that an amendment to the petition has been filed, which should be on DE-120.

Code sec. 12200 two weeks before the hearing date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD JOSEPH FRENETTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/19/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (3) The date of death must be specific for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D.] (4) The petitioner, the person named in item 2 of the petition, is missing from item 8 of the petition. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/5/25. (A supplement, prepared on pleading papers and signed and verified by the petitioner, should suffice.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DON RAYMOND SCHMITZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/29/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1) Item 3a is incomplete (whether decedent was a resident of SF County). 2) Petitioner cites Probate Code 6124 but does not address whether testator had the will in his possession and was competent until death. If so, the presumption does arise, and Petitioner will need to rebut the presumption. 3) Petitioner alleges that Decedent had a spouse at the time of his death. Please confirm and allege date of marriage, as the will indicates that Decedent never married or was in a registered domestic partnership. 4) Notice filed on 6/18/25 does not indicate that IAEA authority is being requested. It also does not indicate that the will being requested to be admitted is lost. The notice must be re-sent for the continued hearing date. 5) DE-147S has not been filed. 6) A copy of the lost will must be filed in the same way as an original will is filed.
